# ADR-260033: Geometric Closest-Point Bond Anchoring for Network View

## Workflow

- This ADR records the accepted anchoring refinement for Network-view bonds.
- It refines ADR-260029 by replacing side-only nearest-handle attachment with geometric closest-point circle-boundary anchoring.
- The objective is improved legibility for medium/large atom structures while preserving data and view boundaries.

Status: Accepted
Date: 2026-05-13
Owners: Repository owner, product collaborator

## Context

The Network view currently follows a Neo4j-like circle-node model with explicit connector affordance. In denser graph structures, fixed-side handle attachment produces avoidable crossings and visual clutter.

A geometric boundary anchoring rule is needed so bond attachment points remain visually meaningful as topology grows.

## Decision

### D1: Geometric Boundary Anchoring
- Network-view bonds attach to the closest boundary point on each source/target circle based on current node geometry.
- The attachment rule is geometric and continuous (not limited to side-only top/right/bottom/left approximations).

### D2: Recompute Triggers
- Anchors are recomputed whenever node geometry changes (drag, layout reflow, resize).
- Zoom/pan changes do not alter anchoring semantics.

### D3: Scope Boundary Preservation
- This decision applies only to Network view rendering.
- Flow view behavior and backend bond data model remain unchanged.

### D4: Label Extensibility Seam
- Network-view bond labels may be omitted by default to reduce clutter.
- Edge label data remains preserved so labels can be enabled later without contract changes.

## Alternatives Considered

- Option A: Keep side-handle anchoring only. Rejected: degrades readability for dense structures.
- Option B: Use nearest of four side handles as approximation. Rejected for baseline: may still produce visible artifacts compared with true geometric anchoring.
- Option C: Add labels first and defer anchoring refinement. Rejected: amplifies clutter before baseline readability is fixed.

## Consequences

### Positive
- Improves bond readability and spatial interpretation in dense views.
- Preserves existing data contracts and Flow view behavior.
- Keeps a clean future seam for optional label rendering.

### Negative
- Requires geometry-aware edge mapping logic.
- Adds testing surface for anchoring correctness and recomputation behavior.

### Risks
- Poorly tuned recompute logic may impact interaction smoothness on larger graphs.
- Edge-overlap edge cases (parallel/multi-edges, very short distances) may need follow-up polish.

## Verification

- Unit tests validate geometric anchor-point computation against representative node-position scenarios.
- Integration tests validate anchor recomputation after node drag and layout updates.
- E2E tests validate visual nearest-boundary attachment for loaded and newly created bonds.
- Regression checks validate Flow view behavior remains unchanged.

## Rollback

- If performance/regression risk is unacceptable, temporarily roll back to side-approximation anchoring while preserving the connector interaction model.
- Keep label-extensibility seam unchanged regardless of anchoring rollback.
